# cardano-crypto

> Pure Rust implementation of Cardano cryptographic primitives with 100% binary compatibility with IntersectMBO/cardano-node

## Overview

This crate provides production-grade implementations of Cardano cryptographic primitives used in the Cardano blockchain. All implementations are byte-for-byte compatible with the official Haskell cardano-base library.

**Core Primitives:**
- VRF (Verifiable Random Functions): ECVRF-ED25519-SHA512-Elligator2 Draft-03 and Draft-13
- KES (Key Evolving Signatures): Sum and Single KES with compact variants
- DSIGN (Digital Signatures): Ed25519, secp256k1 (ECDSA + Schnorr)
- Hash Functions: Blake2b (224, 256, 512), SHA-256, SHA-512
- BLS12-381: Pairing-friendly curve operations (Plutus)
- HD Wallets: BIP32-Ed25519 derivation (CIP-1852)

**Standards Compliance:**
- IETF VRF Draft-03, Draft-13
- RFC 8032 (Ed25519)
- RFC 7693 (Blake2b)
- CIP-0049 (secp256k1 in Plutus)
- CIP-0381 (BLS12-381 in Plutus)
- CIP-1852 (HD wallet derivation)

## Quick Start

```rust
use cardano_crypto::{
    vrf::{VrfDraft03, VrfAlgorithm},
    kes::{SumKes6, KesAlgorithm},
    dsign::{Ed25519, DsignAlgorithm},
    hash::Blake2b256,
};

// VRF: Verifiable random function for Cardano Praos consensus
let vrf_seed = [0u8; 32];
let vrf_sk = VrfDraft03::gen_key(&vrf_seed);
let vrf_pk = VrfDraft03::derive_verification_key(&vrf_sk);
let (proof, output) = VrfDraft03::prove(&vrf_sk, b"block_slot_123")?;
VrfDraft03::verify(&vrf_pk, b"block_slot_123", &proof)?;

// KES: Key evolving signatures for block signing
let kes_seed = [0u8; 32];
let kes_sk = SumKes6::gen_key(&kes_seed);
let kes_vk = SumKes6::derive_verification_key(&kes_sk);
let sig = SumKes6::sign(0, &kes_sk, b"block_header")?;
SumKes6::verify(&kes_vk, 0, b"block_header", &sig)?;

// Ed25519: Transaction signatures
let ed_seed = [0u8; 32];
let ed_sk = Ed25519::gen_key(&ed_seed);
let ed_vk = Ed25519::derive_verification_key(&ed_sk);
let sig = Ed25519::sign(&ed_sk, b"transaction_bytes");
Ed25519::verify(&ed_vk, b"transaction_bytes", &sig)?;

// Blake2b: Hash functions
let hash = Blake2b256::hash(b"data_to_hash");
```

## Architecture

**Module Structure:**
```
cardano-crypto/
├── vrf/          # Verifiable Random Functions
│   ├── draft03   # VRF Draft-03 (Cardano Praos)
│   └── draft13   # VRF Draft-13 (batch verification)
├── kes/          # Key Evolving Signatures
│   ├── single    # SingleKES (1 period)
│   └── sum       # SumKES (2^N periods)
├── dsign/        # Digital Signatures
│   ├── ed25519   # Ed25519 (Cardano standard)
│   └── secp256k1 # secp256k1 (Plutus)
├── hash/         # Hash Functions
│   ├── blake2b   # Blake2b-224, 256, 512
│   └── sha       # SHA-256, SHA-512
├── bls/          # BLS12-381 (Plutus)
├── hd/           # HD Wallet Derivation
├── key/          # Key Management
├── seed/         # Seed Derivation
└── cbor/         # CBOR Serialization
```

**Feature Flags:**
```toml
[dependencies]
cardano-crypto = { version = "1.1", features = ["std", "vrf", "kes"] }

# Default features (recommended)
default = ["std", "vrf", "kes", "dsign", "hash", "seed", "cbor", "key", "hd"]

# Minimal features (no_std + alloc)
cardano-crypto = { version = "1.1", default-features = false, features = ["alloc", "vrf"] }

# Plutus features
cardano-crypto = { version = "1.1", features = ["plutus"] }  # Enables secp256k1 + BLS
```

## Key Concepts

### 1. Cardano Compatibility

**CRITICAL:** All cryptographic outputs must match IntersectMBO/cardano-base byte-for-byte.

- VRF proofs use Elligator2 mapping (Cardano-specific libsodium compatibility)
- KES uses Sum composition with compact serialization
- All test vectors verified against cardano-base golden tests

### 2. Security

**Secret Key Management:**
```rust
use zeroize::Zeroize;

// Keys are automatically zeroized on drop
let mut secret_key = Ed25519::gen_key(&seed);
// ... use key ...
// Automatically zeroized when dropped

// Manual zeroization if needed
secret_key.zeroize();
```

**Constant-Time Operations:**
```rust
use subtle::ConstantTimeEq;

// Use constant-time comparisons for secrets
let is_valid = tag.ct_eq(&expected_tag).into();
```

### 3. KES Period Management

KES keys evolve over time with periods:

```rust
use cardano_crypto::kes::{SumKes6, KesAlgorithm};

let mut sk = SumKes6::gen_key(&seed);
let vk = SumKes6::derive_verification_key(&sk);

// Sign at period 0
let sig0 = SumKes6::sign(0, &sk, b"block_0")?;

// Update key to period 1 (irreversible!)
SumKes6::update(&mut sk, 1)?;

// Sign at period 1
let sig1 = SumKes6::sign(1, &sk, b"block_1")?;

// Cannot sign for period 0 anymore (forward security)
```

**KES Variants:**
- `SumKes6` - 64 periods (2^6), standard for Cardano
- `SumKes7` - 128 periods (2^7)
- Compact variants reduce key size

### 4. VRF Usage Patterns

**Slot Leader Election:**
```rust
use cardano_crypto::vrf::{VrfDraft03, VrfAlgorithm};

fn check_slot_leadership(
    vrf_sk: &VrfSigningKey,
    slot: u64,
    epoch_nonce: &[u8],
    stake_ratio: f64,
) -> Option<VrfProof> {
    let message = format!("slot_{}_nonce_{:?}", slot, epoch_nonce);
    let (proof, output) = VrfDraft03::prove(vrf_sk, message.as_bytes()).ok()?;
    
    // Check if output meets difficulty threshold
    let hash_value = u64::from_le_bytes(output[..8].try_into().ok()?);
    let threshold = (u64::MAX as f64 * stake_ratio) as u64;
    
    if hash_value < threshold {
        Some(proof)
    } else {
        None
    }
}
```

### 5. HD Wallet Derivation

**CIP-1852 Address Derivation:**
```rust
use cardano_crypto::hd::{derive_account_key, derive_address_key};

// m/1852'/1815'/0' (account)
let root_key = /* ... */;
let account_key = derive_account_key(&root_key, 0)?;

// m/1852'/1815'/0'/0/0 (external address)
let address_key = derive_address_key(&account_key, 0, 0)?;

// m/1852'/1815'/0'/1/0 (internal change address)
let change_key = derive_address_key(&account_key, 1, 0)?;
```

## Common Patterns

### Transaction Signing

```rust
use cardano_crypto::{
    dsign::{Ed25519, DsignAlgorithm},
    hash::Blake2b256,
};

fn sign_transaction(sk: &Ed25519SigningKey, tx_body: &[u8]) -> Ed25519Signature {
    // Hash transaction body first
    let tx_hash = Blake2b256::hash(tx_body);
    
    // Sign the hash
    Ed25519::sign(sk, &tx_hash)
}

fn verify_transaction(
    vk: &Ed25519VerificationKey,
    tx_body: &[u8],
    sig: &Ed25519Signature,
) -> Result<()> {
    let tx_hash = Blake2b256::hash(tx_body);
    Ed25519::verify(vk, &tx_hash, sig)
}
```

### Block Production

```rust
use cardano_crypto::{
    vrf::{VrfDraft03, VrfAlgorithm},
    kes::{SumKes6, KesAlgorithm},
};

struct BlockHeader {
    slot: u64,
    vrf_proof: VrfProof,
    kes_signature: KesSignature,
    // ... other fields
}

fn produce_block(
    vrf_sk: &VrfSigningKey,
    kes_sk: &mut KesSigningKey,
    kes_period: u64,
    slot: u64,
    epoch_nonce: &[u8],
) -> Result<BlockHeader> {
    // VRF proof for slot leadership
    let slot_msg = format!("slot_{}_nonce_{:?}", slot, epoch_nonce);
    let (vrf_proof, _output) = VrfDraft03::prove(vrf_sk, slot_msg.as_bytes())?;
    
    // Build header bytes
    let header_bytes = /* ... construct header ... */;
    
    // KES signature
    let kes_sig = SumKes6::sign(kes_period, kes_sk, &header_bytes)?;
    
    Ok(BlockHeader {
        slot,
        vrf_proof,
        kes_signature: kes_sig,
    })
}
```

### Plutus Script Verification

```rust
#[cfg(feature = "plutus")]
use cardano_crypto::{
    dsign::secp256k1::{Secp256k1, verify_ecdsa},
    bls::{G1Point, G2Point, pairing},
};

// ECDSA verification (CIP-0049)
fn verify_plutus_ecdsa(
    pubkey: &[u8],
    message: &[u8],
    signature: &[u8],
) -> Result<bool> {
    verify_ecdsa(pubkey, message, signature)
}

// BLS signature verification (CIP-0381)
fn verify_plutus_bls(
    pubkey: &G2Point,
    message: &[u8],
    signature: &G1Point,
) -> Result<bool> {
    let hash_point = /* hash message to G1 */;
    let lhs = pairing(signature, &G2Point::generator());
    let rhs = pairing(&hash_point, pubkey);
    Ok(lhs == rhs)
}
```

## Error Handling

**Always use `Result` types:**

```rust
use cardano_crypto::common::{CryptoError, Result};

fn my_function() -> Result<Output> {
    // Validate inputs
    if input.len() != EXPECTED_SIZE {
        return Err(CryptoError::InvalidLength {
            expected: EXPECTED_SIZE,
            actual: input.len(),
        });
    }
    
    // Perform operation
    let result = some_crypto_operation()?;
    Ok(result)
}
```

**Common Errors:**
- `InvalidLength` - Wrong key/signature size
- `InvalidFormat` - Malformed input data
- `VerificationFailed` - Signature/proof verification failed
- `PeriodMismatch` - KES period doesn't match

## Best Practices

### 1. Use Traits for Generic Code

```rust
use cardano_crypto::common::DsignAlgorithm;

fn generic_sign<A: DsignAlgorithm>(
    sk: &A::SigningKey,
    message: &[u8],
) -> A::Signature {
    A::sign(sk, message)
}

// Works with any signature algorithm
let ed_sig = generic_sign::<Ed25519>(&ed_sk, b"msg");
```

### 2. Zeroize Secrets

```rust
use zeroize::Zeroize;

let mut seed = [0u8; 32];
// ... fill seed from secure source ...

// Use seed
let sk = Ed25519::gen_key(&seed);

// Clear seed from memory
seed.zeroize();
```

### 3. Validate All Inputs

```rust
fn process_signature(sig_bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {
    // Validate length first
    if sig_bytes.len() != Ed25519::SIGNATURE_SIZE {
        return Err(CryptoError::InvalidLength {
            expected: Ed25519::SIGNATURE_SIZE,
            actual: sig_bytes.len(),
        });
    }
    
    // Safe to proceed
    let sig = Ed25519Signature::from_bytes(sig_bytes)?;
    Ok(())
}
```

### 4. Use Feature Flags Appropriately

```rust
// Only compile BLS code when plutus feature is enabled
#[cfg(feature = "plutus")]
pub fn verify_bls_aggregate(/* ... */) -> Result<()> {
    // BLS verification
}

// Provide alternative or error when feature is disabled
#[cfg(not(feature = "plutus"))]
pub fn verify_bls_aggregate(/* ... */) -> Result<()> {
    Err(CryptoError::FeatureNotEnabled("plutus"))
}
```

## Testing

**Run all tests:**
```bash
cargo test --all-features
```

**Test specific module:**
```bash
cargo test --features vrf vrf::tests
```

**Test no_std compatibility:**
```bash
cargo test --no-default-features --features alloc,vrf
```

**Property-based tests** are included for all primitives to verify invariants.
